title: [SIM] Define Equipment abstract base class with port interface
body:
## Objective
Define the Equipment abstract base class providing standard lifecycle and port access for all spacecraft equipment models.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Equipment ABC with: equipment_id, initialise(), do_step(), write_port(), read_port()
- PortDirection enum: IN, OUT
- PortDefinition dataclass: name, direction, unit, dtype
- Equipment.declare_ports() class method for port registration
- mypy strict clean
- Unit tests cover port registration and access

## Implements
SVF-DEV-004

title: [SIM] Implement FmuEquipment wrapping FMU as Equipment
body:
## Objective
Wrap an FMI 3.0 FMU as an Equipment implementation, mapping FMU variables to named ports via parameter_map.

## Acceptance Criteria
- FmuEquipment extends Equipment
- FMU inputs exposed as IN ports
- FMU outputs exposed as OUT ports
- write_port() sets FMU input before doStep()
- read_port() reads FMU output after doStep()
- Existing EPS FMU wrappable as FmuEquipment
- Unit tests cover port read/write

## Implements
SVF-DEV-004, SVF-DEV-014

title: [SIM] Implement wiring YAML schema and loader
body:
## Objective
Define a simple human-readable YAML schema for spacecraft equipment wiring and implement a Python loader.

## Schema
```yaml
connections:
  - from: pcdu.bus.lcl1
    to: rw1.power_enable
    description: RW1 power line
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- WiringLoader parses YAML into list of Connection(from, to, description)
- Validates that all referenced equipment_id.port combinations exist
- Duplicate connections raise WiringLoadError
- Unit tests cover valid wiring, missing equipment, duplicates

## Implements
SVF-DEV-004

title: [SIM] Wire Equipment ports via SimulationMaster after each tick
body:
## Objective
Extend SimulationMaster to apply wiring after each tick — copying OUT port values to connected IN ports.

## Design
After all models tick and sync completes, master iterates wiring connections and copies source port values to destination ports via CommandStore.

## Acceptance Criteria
- SimulationMaster accepts optional WiringMap and CommandStore
- After each tick: source OUT port read from ParameterStore, written to CommandStore as destination IN port
- Wiring applied before next tick so receiving model sees value on next step
- Integration test with two connected NativeEquipment models

## Implements
SVF-DEV-004

title: [MOD] Decompose EPS FMU into SolarArray, Battery, PCDU Equipment
body:
## Objective
Refactor the integrated EPS FMU into three separate Equipment instances connected via wiring YAML.

## Models
- SolarArrayEquipment: illumination -> generated_power, array_voltage
- BatteryEquipment: charge_current -> battery_voltage, soc, temperature
- PcduEquipment: solar_power + battery_voltage + load -> bus_voltage + charge_current

## Acceptance Criteria
- Three separate pythonfmu FMUs built and committed
- Wiring YAML in srdb/wiring/eps_wiring.yaml
- Existing TC-PWR-001 through TC-PWR-005 pass unchanged
- SRDB canonical names used throughout

## Implements
SVF-DEV-066
